title: Da Nang 3-Day Beach Getaway
metaTitle: Da Nang 3-Day Beach Getaway | Flights, Hotels & Tours Included
metaDescription: Book a 3-day trip to Da Nang, Vietnam: round-trip flights, hotel, breakfast, transfers, and guided tours including Ba Na Hills and Golden Bridge and Marble Mountains. Prices from $1552 per person (estimate).
highlights: Ba Na Hills and Golden Bridge
Motorbike ride over the Hai Van Pass
overview: Spend 3 days discovering Da Nang, Vietnam — Tropical climate — hot summers (highs around 33-34°C) and a milder, wetter season (Sept-Jan) with a typhoon risk Sept-Nov. February to May — warm, dry weather before the summer heat and autumn typhoon season. This itinerary blends Ba Na Hills and Golden Bridge, Marble Mountains, My Khe Beach with free time to explore at your own pace, backed by round-trip flights, handpicked accommodation, and a dedicated travel consultant from enquiry through departure.
included: Round-trip economy-class airfare from your selected origin city (upgrade to premium economy or business available)
2 nights accommodation in your selected category (Budget, Mid-range, Premium, or Luxury)
Daily local transport as per the itinerary
Guided tours including Ba Na Hills and Golden Bridge, Marble Mountains, My Khe Beach
All entrance fees for attractions and activities listed in the itinerary
Applicable taxes and OTA service fee
Dedicated travel consultant support from booking through departure
notIncluded: Visa fees and travel document costs (see Visa Information)
Travel insurance (strongly recommended, available as an add-on)
Lunches and dinners except where noted in the itinerary
Optional activities not listed in the day-by-day itinerary
Airline seat selection and baggage upgrades
Any fare difference for premium economy or business class upgrades

itinerary.title: Arrive in Da Nang
itinerary.description: Land at Da Nang International Airport (DAD) and take your included private transfer to your hotel. After settling in, ease into the trip with a relaxed visit to Ba Na Hills and Golden Bridge — Mountaintop resort reached by cable car, famous for the stone "hands" bridge. Round off the evening with a welcome dinner introducing you to local flavors.
activities: Ba Na Hills and Golden Bridge

itinerary.title: Da Nang: Marble Mountains & Hoi An Ancient Town entry
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Marble Mountains — Cluster of marble/limestone hills with cave pagodas and viewpoints. In the afternoon, continue to Hoi An Ancient Town entry — UNESCO-listed lantern-lit old town, a short trip from Da Nang. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Departure Day
itinerary.description: Enjoy a final morning at leisure — perhaps one last visit to My Son Sanctuary or some souvenir shopping — before your private transfer to DAD for your departure flight.

travelTips: Base yourself in Da Nang or Hoi An and day-trip between the two — they're only 30 minutes apart.
Visit Ba Na Hills on a clear day, since the Golden Bridge views are weather-dependent.
Time a Hoi An evening visit around the monthly full-moon Lantern Festival for the most atmospheric experience.
Avoid the September-November typhoon season if flexible on travel dates.
With a short stay, book your must-see attractions in advance to avoid losing time to queues.
packingList: Copies of booking confirmations and travel insurance
Lightweight, breathable clothing and a reusable water bottle
Modest clothing for temples and My Son Sanctuary
Sturdy shoes for Marble Mountains' cave stairs
thingsToDo: Ba Na Hills and Golden Bridge
Motorbike ride over the Hai Van Pass
thingsToAvoid: Riding a motorbike over the Hai Van Pass without prior experience — the road has serious drop-offs and traffic.
Visiting My Son Sanctuary at midday — it's shadeless and hot.
Booking outdoor activities during the September-November typhoon season without flexibility.
topRestaurants: Nha Hang Ba Thoi — Vietnamese seafood ($$)
Bep Nha Minh Duc — Local Vietnamese home-style ($)
instagramSpots: Golden Bridge stone hands at Ba Na Hills
hiddenGems: Son Tra Peninsula and the Lady Buddha statue
adventureActivities: Motorbike ride over the Hai Van Pass
luxuryExperiences: Beachfront resort villa along My Khe or Non Nuoc Beach
Private Hoi An and My Son day tour with a driver
Cable car and dining experience at Ba Na Hills
honeymoonExperiences: Private lantern-lit Hoi An evening (day trip)
Beachfront villa with a private pool
budgetExperiences: Dragon Bridge weekend fire show viewing

festivalCalendar.name: Hoi An Lantern Festival (monthly full moon, nearby)

localEtiquette: Modest dress is appreciated at temples and pagodas.
Bargaining is common in markets, less so in fixed-price shops and restaurants.
A slight bow or nod is a polite greeting; handshakes are also common in business contexts.
localTransportGuide: Grab rideshare (car and motorbike) is the easiest way to get around Da Nang and to Hoi An. Renting a motorbike is popular for the scenic Hai Van Pass, though traffic norms differ from home and caution is advised for inexperienced riders.
bestTimeToVisit: February to May — warm, dry weather before the summer heat and autumn typhoon season.
weather: Tropical climate — hot summers (highs around 33-34°C) and a milder, wetter season (Sept-Jan) with a typhoon risk Sept-Nov.
visaInformation: Vietnam requires an e-visa for most nationalities including the US, UK, EU, Canada, Australia, and India, arranged online before travel; some European nationalities have short-stay visa exemptions.
averageFlightTime: ~10.3h from major hubs (estimate; varies by origin — see Suggested Flights)
cancellationPolicy: Cancellations made 30+ days before departure: full refund minus a $99 processing fee. 15-29 days before departure: 50% refund. 0-14 days before departure or no-show: non-refundable. Airline and hotel supplier cancellation terms may be stricter than this policy and apply in addition — your travel consultant confirms exact terms before payment is taken. No online payment is collected on this site; a consultant confirms final pricing and availability with you directly before any booking is confirmed.
termsAndConditions: Pricing shown is per person based on the traveler type and tier selected and is an estimate pending live fare and rate confirmation.
Passports must be valid for at least 6 months beyond the return date for most destinations — confirm the exact requirement for your nationality.
Itinerary order may change due to weather, local conditions, or attraction closures; inclusions remain equivalent.
Solo travelers incur a single-room supplement, reflected in the Solo pricing tier.
This package does not include international travel insurance unless explicitly listed under Included.
Prices shown in a currency other than USD are approximate conversions at the rate noted and are not a payment quote.

faqs.question: Do I need a visa to visit Da Nang?
faqs.answer: Vietnam requires an e-visa for most nationalities including the US, UK, EU, Canada, Australia, and India, arranged online before travel; some European nationalities have short-stay visa exemptions.

faqs.question: What is the best time to visit Da Nang?
faqs.answer: February to May — warm, dry weather before the summer heat and autumn typhoon season. Tropical climate — hot summers (highs around 33-34°C) and a milder, wetter season (Sept-Jan) with a typhoon risk Sept-Nov.

faqs.question: What's included in this package?
faqs.answer: Round-trip airfare, accommodation, daily breakfast, airport transfers, daily local transport, and guided tours to the attractions listed in the itinerary. See the Included / Not Included sections for full detail.

faqs.question: Can the itinerary be customized?
faqs.answer: Yes — this itinerary is a starting point. Your travel consultant can add, remove, or reorder activities, extend your stay, or upgrade your hotel category before you book.

faqs.question: What is the cancellation policy?
faqs.answer: Cancellations made 30+ days before departure: full refund minus a $99 processing fee. 15-29 days before departure: 50% refund. 0-14 days before departure or no-show: non-refundable. Airline and hotel supplier cancellation terms may be stricter than this policy and apply in addition — your travel consultant confirms exact terms before payment is taken. No online payment is collected on this site; a consultant confirms final pricing and availability with you directly before any booking is confirmed.

faqs.question: Is Da Nang safe for tourists?
faqs.answer: Da Nang is rated very safe for tourists, with low rates of violent crime. Usual travel precautions (watch belongings in crowds, use licensed taxis) still apply.

faqs.question: What currency should I bring to Da Nang?
faqs.answer: The local currency is VND. Cards and mobile payments are widely accepted, and ATMs are easy to find.

faqs.question: Is this package good for a honeymoon or family trip?
faqs.answer: It also works well for families — highlights include Ba Na Hills Fantasy Park.
